The second thing here is that every single mass movement had is they had a cause. They had something bigger than themselves that they were offering their audience. It was something bigger about that was happening in the future. What's this thing that's happening in the future? The future-based cause. That's what we're going to kind of break down right now. There's different pieces of this. I'm going to go through all of them. The first thing I want to share is, in fact, I think I got a cool quote in here I wanted to share. Okay. And then later in the movie, when she's kind of, and we're talking about story stuff right now, when she has had her transformation in the story and she's coming to the rebellion and she's talking to him and she's trying to pitch them, like we need to go fight or we're all going to be destroyed. And this is our last shot. And they come back to her and they're like, if we try that, we're going to lose. Like there's no way we can win. And she's like, all we have is hope, rebellions are built on hope. And that becomes like the rallying cry, right? And that then takes them to go and go into war and it's awesome. Yeah, I should all watch it. Most movies have this, right? Like think about Braveheart when Mel Gibson comes out and does this thing about freedom. Like there's this thing that we're doing to get people to stand up and want to do something, right? When you're building a movement, you're trying to get people something that they can put their faith and their hope into, okay? Because a lot of times they don't have faith in themselves. And so there's got to be something which is coming back to you, like something else that bigger than them that they can say, look, I don't know if I can do this, but man, he's doing it. She's doing it. I can do, like this is something that I can become part of. And as I was doing this section of the book and writing it, it was interesting. It was in the midst of the elections when there was all this drama and hatred and stuff that was flying. And I was just kind of sitting back and watching this. And so I started looking back. I was like, I typed in presidential slogans. And I wanted to see the slogans. And when I did, it was like the most fascinating, interesting thing going back through time. So in 1992, George Bush's tradition was, or George Bush's campaign slogan was a proud tradition. Okay? He was competing against Bill Clinton. Bill Clinton's was, it's time to change America. Okay? Which one is like, that's the future I want. Okay? Bill Clinton won. Okay? In 2008, John McCain said, John McCain, country first. Okay? Barack Obama came out and said, we can change. Yes we can change. 2016, you guys remember Hillary's quote was, ugh, I'm with her. The other one she had was stronger together. And what was Donald Trump's? I think of all the ones I've ever seen, Donald Trump's is the best I've ever seen. Make America great again. That's the future. That's the one I want to go. Again, yeah. Right? Isn't that cool? Like how powerful, like all these people, as far back as I could go, and like the loser always had something about themselves, about the present, and all the winners said about the future. Not saying it's the only thing that affects politics, but I don't know. It's interesting. If you can cast a compelling future that people can follow you into, like that's so huge, so powerful. And so for us, like I didn't know, like, you know, these are things like I didn't know we needed them or won them or how powerful it worked until we created it. Like we went out there and before this year's full hacking live, so we, I'd gotten the video back from Dan. I don't know if Dan's still in here, but Dan, Dan's the one that built the video for a full hacking live. You guys have seen that video. It's like, it's amazing. So I watched the video and I was just like, God, like I watched it like 20 times and I was so inspired by it. I was like, this is the coolest thing in the world. And I sat down and I'm like, I'm gonna write the sales letter to get people to come to our next event. And I was like, what's, like, how do I, like, what's that thing that like people are like, like, why are they coming to this event? Like, why are they leaving their homes, spending good money to fly somewhere? Like, what's the vision? Like, why am I doing this? I don't want to like, you come to this event, we're gonna hang out, I'm gonna sell you some stuff. Like, that's stupid. That doesn't inspire anything. Like, what's, what's the future we're trying to paint? And so we sat down and, and Stephen was in the room, John, but I'm gonna show you guys we're in there and like, I wrote this headline. It was like, you're just one funnel away from being financially free. I said, we guys think and they're like, huh? Everyone's gonna go, I'm like, okay, well, that sucks. I'm like, well, you're one funnel away from firing your boss. I was like, some people probably like that, but like, not everyone. I'm like, well, you're one funnel away from having more time with your kids. You're one funnel away from like getting your message out and sharing it with the world. You're one funnel, and we had all these different things and all of them like, were kind of good. They all touched somebody. And I was like, all angry. So I like, at the end I deleted the end and I deleted it and I'm thinking, like, trying to get the next thing. And I look up at the screen and I see it's just this. You're just one funnel away. And I was like, whoa. I'm like, that's it. I'm like, done. I'm like, dude, that's it. I was like, think about this. Like for everyone, it's different, right? Like everyone of you guys have a different mission. Like you're for different purposes. And for me, it was like, you're one funnel away. Like that could be anything for any of you guys. And I have so many stories at times that I have completely crashed my business and like one funnel. I've never gone through bankruptcy, bank heavens. Three times I should have, but three times I was saved by a funnel. Like that's not a joke. That's a complete truth. And like for me, it was like one funnel away saving me from bankruptcy, helped me get my mess, like all these different things that happened in life. And different points in my life, it was different things. But and so I put it out there. We started doing it. And I remember two or three days later, I saw a guy in our Facebook group post something. And he said, he said, he said, I was going to quit this whole internet marketing thing. I've done four or five funnels. Nothing's working. I'm doing this over a year. I'm all depressed. And I saw Russell's new thing. And I read that. And he said, I think Russell's trying to tell me something. If I keep doing this, eventually it's going to work. He said, I'm doubling that down. I'm going to do this. And I was like, that's it. That's what I want. I want this vision for people. And so and now like you watch all my videos, sometimes I forget, but I tried it out. All my emails and my PS, don't forget you're just one funnel away. And my videos like, hey, I finished my video. Like don't forget you're just one funnel away. I try to say it all the time. Everyone's saying that and thinking that over and over and over again. Funlacking live and you have to show up. You're all going to shirt since you're one funnel away.
